iterations.strategy: Root cause is a plan/physical mismatch: iter_0 spends 321ms in build_joins by constructing large runtime hash tables (pre triple counts and tag PK map) that collectively exceed LLC and repeat work already representable by existing posting indexes. Strategy Category A (plan restructuring) with hardware-aware simplification: use pre_stmt_hash as the selective driver and probe num_adsh_tag_version_hash directly (empirically fastest join order from sampling), then apply num filters, sub PK SIC filter, and late tag join before aggregation; this removes the dominant pre runtime build path and shifts work to index-driven probes proportional to filtered rows.

Root cause is a hardware/physical mismatch (Category B): iter_1 pays most time in startup by deserializing and touching cache-exceeding posting indexes (notably num_adsh_tag_version_hash) and rebuilding tag lookup state, so performance is dominated by memory traffic before useful filtering. The revised plan removes those large index-driven probes and uses a fused sequential num scan with early selective filters plus two compact cache-friendlier hash structures: a pre(stmt='EQ') triple semi-join set and a tag(tag,version)->tlabel map, with sub SIC filtering applied first via dense lookup. This shifts work to sequential access and proportional hash probes, reducing wasted startup work and random-access pressure.

Root cause is wasted work and startup-heavy physical choices: the prior plan deserializes and probes cache-exceeding triple index structures before the selective SIC predicate is applied, so data_loading and build_joins dominate. Strategy Category C (work elimination) with plan restructuring applies sub SIC filtering first, intersects pre(stmt='EQ') with those adsh values to build a much smaller triple semi-join set, then probes num through num_adsh_fk_hash only for surviving filings; tag lookup is materialized only for needed (tag,version) keys. This removes most unnecessary startup parsing and reduces probe work to rows proportional to filtered adsh output.

Root cause is residual wasted work in the hot path (Category C): main_scan still performs per-row tag lookups and repeated DISTINCT-cik insert attempts for the same adsh/group, while build_joins eagerly materializes tag mappings before knowing surviving aggregate keys. The revised plan keeps the selective sub+pre pruning and num_adsh index probing, but switches to a two-phase aggregation keyed by (sic,tag,version,stmt), records DISTINCT cik once per adsh-group, and resolves tlabel only for post-scan distinct (tag,version) keys with abstract=0 before final rollup to (sic,tlabel,stmt). This removes redundant per-row hash/set work and shifts tag join cost to work proportional to surviving groups.

Root cause is a combined physical/work mismatch: the current hot path still does per-row hash work in main_scan and resolves tag labels through a serialized PK map that causes random rowid accesses and cache-miss-heavy probes. Strategy Category B/C changes the plan to consolidate qualifying num rows per adsh into a compact (tag,version) map before pre multiplicity probes, reducing repeated hash lookups and DISTINCT insert attempts, then resolves tlabel by scanning tag columns sequentially for only needed keys to replace random-access PK-map probing. This keeps selective sub+pre pruning while shifting the remaining heavy work to sequential, cache-friendlier passes proportional to surviving groups.
